title: On Miss Jessy Lewars.
content:
Say, sages, what's the charm on earth
            Can turn Death's dart aside?
        It is not purity and worth,
            Else Jessy had not died.

    R. B.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "On Miss Jessy Lewars.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
